channel.title: TechNode
channel.description: Latest news and trends about tech in China

item.title: ByteDance 2025 profit drops over 70% amid surging AI investment
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: According to sources, ByteDance’s net profit in 2025 declined by more than 70% year-on-year, with its net profit margin also seeing a significant drop. This was attributed to the company’s substantial increase in investment in AI businesses during the third and fourth quarters of last year. At the same time, ByteDance’s overseas revenue in 2025 […]
item.content:encoded:
According to sources, ByteDance’s net profit in 2025 declined by more than 70% year-on-year, with its net profit margin also seeing a significant drop. This was attributed to the company’s substantial increase in investment in AI businesses during the third and fourth quarters of last year. At the same time, ByteDance’s overseas revenue in 2025 grew by nearly 50%, far outpacing the roughly 20% growth in its domestic business.

The share of overseas revenue in the company’s revenue rose from 25% in 2024 to over 30%. TikTok Shop’s gross merchandise value (GMV) in 2025 increased by nearly 70% year-on-year, becoming the main driver behind the rising share of ByteDance’s overseas revenue. Currently, ByteDance did not comment on the above figures. [Jiemian, in Chinese]

item.title: Xiaomi miclaw among first approved in China CAICT Claw evaluation
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Xiaomi said its Xiaomi miclaw mobile intelligent agent has become one of the first systems to pass the China Academy of Information and Communications Technology (CAICT) Claw smartphone intelligent assistant evaluation, marking a regulatory approval for advanced on-device AI systems. The Chinese smartphone maker said Xiaomi miclaw is powered by its in-house Xiaomi MiMo large […]
item.content:encoded:
Xiaomi said its Xiaomi miclaw mobile intelligent agent has become one of the first systems to pass the China Academy of Information and Communications Technology (CAICT) Claw smartphone intelligent assistant evaluation, marking a regulatory approval for advanced on-device AI systems. The Chinese smartphone maker said Xiaomi miclaw is powered by its in-house Xiaomi MiMo large language model and a device-side priority architecture, enabling what it described as four core capabilities: ecosystem-wide integration, deep memory understanding, cross-domain connectivity, and continuous self-improvement.

According to Xiaomi, the system can link smartphones, PCs, in-car platforms and AIoT devices, and is capable of autonomously executing complex user instructions across different environments. The company said Xiaomi miclaw is currently being rolled out in a limited testing programme for technology enthusiasts and advanced users. [TechNode Reporting]

item.title: Honor’s humanoid robot breaks human half marathon record in 50min 26sec
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: On Sunday, at the Beijing Yizhuang Humanoid Robot Half Marathon, Honor’s “Flash” robot won the championship with a time of 50 minutes and 26 seconds, setting a new record and surpassing the human half marathon world record of 56 minutes and 42 seconds. The course measured 21.0975 kilometers and featured a variety of complex terrains. […]
item.content:encoded: On Sunday, at the Beijing Yizhuang Humanoid Robot Half Marathon, Honor’s “Flash” robot won the championship with a time of 50 minutes and 26 seconds, setting a new record and surpassing the human half marathon world record of 56 minutes and 42 seconds. The course measured 21.0975 kilometers and featured a variety of complex terrains. The “Flash” robot stands 169cm tall and adopts a sleek, mech-inspired design. It is equipped with autonomous perception and navigation capabilities, along with a self-developed high-dynamic motion system. [TechNode Reporting]

item.title: China authority says daily AI token usage exceeds 140 trillion in March, up over 40% vs end-2025
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: China’s daily average token usage exceeded 140 trillion in March, up more than 40% from the end of 2025, a senior statistics official said on Thursday, underscoring rapid progress in the commercialisation and large-scale deployment of AI. Mao Shengyong, deputy head of the National Bureau of Statistics, told a State Council Information Office briefing that […]
item.content:encoded:
China’s daily average token usage exceeded 140 trillion in March, up more than 40% from the end of 2025, a senior statistics official said on Thursday, underscoring rapid progress in the commercialisation and large-scale deployment of AI. Mao Shengyong, deputy head of the National Bureau of Statistics, told a State Council Information Office briefing that China has made phased breakthroughs in scaling AI applications across industries.

The expansion of AI is boosting growth in related sectors. In the first quarter, value-added output of digital product manufacturing above designated size rose 11.2% from a year earlier, Mao said. Output in industries directly tied to AI production and use grew sharply, with electronic special materials manufacturing up 32.5% and integrated circuit manufacturing rising 49.4%, he added. [China Star Market, in Chinese]

item.title: Tesla considers humanoid robot production at Shanghai Gigafactory
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Tesla is considering producing humanoid robots at its Shanghai Gigafactory. The plant has been manufacturing electric vehicles since 2019 and began producing large-scale energy storage batteries in 2025. The new plan would expand its manufacturing scope to include humanoid robots, aiming to sustain the company’s growth momentum in the Chinese market. Wang Hao, President of […]
item.content:encoded:
Tesla is considering producing humanoid robots at its Shanghai Gigafactory. The plant has been manufacturing electric vehicles since 2019 and began producing large-scale energy storage batteries in 2025. The new plan would expand its manufacturing scope to include humanoid robots, aiming to sustain the company’s growth momentum in the Chinese market.

Wang Hao, President of Tesla China, said that as the era of robotics arrives, the Shanghai Gigafactory, like Tesla’s other factories, will play a crucial role as a major production base and contribute to products such as robots. Tesla has not disclosed details regarding the timeline or production scale of the potential project. [Jiemian, in Chinese]

item.title: Claude puts up a wall as ID checks complicate access for Chinese users
item.dc:creator: Jessie Wu
item.description: AI tool Claude, developed by Anthropic, suddenly announced the rollout of a new identity verification system requiring users to complete a real-time selfie check while holding a government-issued ID. The move has drawn global attention, but for Chinese users in particular, it feels like a heavy blow that erects a difficult-to-cross “wall” in AI access. […]
item.content:encoded:
AI tool Claude, developed by Anthropic, suddenly announced the rollout of a new identity verification system requiring users to complete a real-time selfie check while holding a government-issued ID.

The move has drawn global attention, but for Chinese users in particular, it feels like a heavy blow that erects a difficult-to-cross “wall” in AI access.

This verification is not being applied universally to all users at once. Instead, it is being introduced gradually in specific scenarios. When users attempt to access certain advanced features, or as part of routine platform integrity checks and other safety and compliance measures, a verification prompt may appear.

The process itself appears simple and typically takes no more than five minutes. However, users must prepare a government-issued photo ID—such as a passport, driver’s license, or national ID card—and use a camera-enabled device to capture a real-time selfie.

For Chinese users, the impact of this mechanism is both broad and profound. The barrier to entry has been significantly raised: individuals without passports are excluded from using Claude.

Even for those who do have passports, older accounts may become valuable assets, while new users face hurdles due to real-name verification requirements, making normal access increasingly difficult.

Submitting passport images and live facial data to a US-based company raises serious concerns around data security and privacy, especially amid a complex geopolitical environment. If such sensitive information were to be compromised, the consequences could be difficult to predict.

Claude’s move may only be the beginning. If proven effective, other AI companies could follow suit, fundamentally reshaping the ecosystem of overseas AI services.

Access for Chinese users to high-quality international AI tools may narrow further, and global AI competition could shift from open competition toward regional fragmentation.

This development also serves as a wake-up call and a potential opening for domestic AI models. Chinese AI developers should seize this moment to accelerate technological innovation and product optimization, improve user experience, and better meet growing domestic demand for AI tools, ultimately reducing reliance on overseas AI services.

item.title: Tesla completes AI5 chip tape-out, to be manufactured by TSMC and Samsung
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk said the company has completed the tape-out of its next-generation AI chip, AI5. He also thanked TSMC and Samsung, which will be responsible for producing the chip. On Wednesday, Musk said, “Congratulations to Tesla’s AI chip design team on completing the tape-out of the AI5 chip.” He added, “We are developing […]
item.content:encoded:
Tesla CEO Elon Musk said the company has completed the tape-out of its next-generation AI chip, AI5. He also thanked TSMC and Samsung, which will be responsible for producing the chip. On Wednesday, Musk said, “Congratulations to Tesla’s AI chip design team on completing the tape-out of the AI5 chip.” He added, “We are developing exciting chips, including AI6 and Dojo3.” Tape-out means that the chip design has been finalized and handed over for manufacturing.

Musk also said to TSMC and Samsung, which will produce the AI5 chip, “Thank you for your support in production. This chip will become one of the highest-volume AI chips in history.” According to sources, full-scale mass production is expected in 2027, roughly about a year after the tape-out is completed. These chips will be used in Tesla’s products such as autonomous driving vehicles and humanoid robots. [Jiwei, in Chinese]

item.title: JD.com launches robot ambulance service, plans expansion to 50+ cities across China
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: JD.com on Wednesday launched its robot ambulance service, offering maintenance and repair solutions for humanoid robots, quadruped robots, AI companion robots, and more. The service covers basic repairs, fault diagnosis, battery replacement and recharging, testing and certification, cosmetic maintenance, and equipment recycling. Currently, JD.com’s robot ambulance is available in the Beijing area. Over the next […]
item.content:encoded: JD.com on Wednesday launched its robot ambulance service, offering maintenance and repair solutions for humanoid robots, quadruped robots, AI companion robots, and more. The service covers basic repairs, fault diagnosis, battery replacement and recharging, testing and certification, cosmetic maintenance, and equipment recycling. Currently, JD.com’s robot ambulance is available in the Beijing area. Over the next three years, its on-site robot repair service will expand to more than 50 major cities across China. [Jiemian, in Chinese]

item.title: DJI to launch Lito X1 series on April 23, likely to feature 360-degree obstacle avoidance system
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Chinese drone maker DJI said it will launch its Lito X1 series on April 23, marking the debut of what is expected to be its first new consumer drone line this year. The model, which has appeared in filings with the FCC under the code SS3-DGP14, is widely expected to succeed the Mini 5 Pro […]
item.content:encoded:
Chinese drone maker DJI said it will launch its Lito X1 series on April 23, marking the debut of what is expected to be its first new consumer drone line this year. The model, which has appeared in filings with the FCC under the code SS3-DGP14, is widely expected to succeed the Mini 5 Pro and target advanced imaging users, according to people familiar with the matter. The Lito X1 is expected to feature DJI’s next-generation O5 transmission system, with a range of more than 20 km. It could also integrate 5G cellular connectivity through software-defined radio technology, the sources said.

For safety, the drone is likely to include a 360-degree obstacle avoidance system combining fisheye cameras, LiDAR-like sensors with a detection range of up to 10 meters, and infrared modules. It may also support voice commands and gesture-based controls. [IThome, in Chinese]

item.title: Alibaba’s Amap to launch first embodied robot, starting with robotic dog
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Alibaba’s subsidiary Amap (Gaode) is set to release its first product from its embodied intelligence division—a quadruped robot. This is reportedly the first embodied robot product introduced by Alibaba Group. According to insiders, humanoid robots and robotic dogs are both key directions being explored by Amap’s embodied intelligence team. The company has also built what […]
item.content:encoded: Alibaba’s subsidiary Amap (Gaode) is set to release its first product from its embodied intelligence division—a quadruped robot. This is reportedly the first embodied robot product introduced by Alibaba Group. According to insiders, humanoid robots and robotic dogs are both key directions being explored by Amap’s embodied intelligence team. The company has also built what is said to be the industry’s largest embodied navigation data engine, and has made progress in handling long-horizon, complex tasks. This move signals that Alibaba’s AI strategy is extending from the digital world into the physical world. [IThome, in Chinese]
